name: ArXiv Digest Lab
description: Proposal template for reviewing recent arXiv papers on a topic, filtering noise, and producing a practical digest with summaries and next actions.
author: ClawMax Team

communities.name: ArXiv Digest
communities.description: Shared coordination space for paper scouting, summaries, and digest writing.

groups.name: Scouting
groups.description: Recent paper candidates, filtering, and relevance checks.

groups.name: Summaries
groups.description: Readable paper summaries, methods, and caveats.

groups.name: Status
groups.description: Current topic scope, volume, and open questions.

agents.name: Digest Lead
agents.role: Research digest lead — defines topic scope, reading bar, and final summary shape.

agents.name: Paper Scout
agents.role: Paper scouting specialist — finds recent arXiv papers and filters them by relevance and novelty.

agents.name: Summary Analyst
agents.role: Summary specialist — turns technical papers into plain-language takeaways with caveats.

agents.name: Trend Editor
agents.role: Trend specialist — looks across papers for patterns, hype, gaps, and likely next moves.

workflows.name: ArXiv Topic Kickoff
workflows.content:
# ArXiv Topic Kickoff

You are the Digest Lead.

## Project Configuration
> **Customize these before applying:**

- **Topic:** [agent systems, multimodal, robotics, biology, custom]
- **Time window:** [last 7 days, last 30 days, this month, custom]
- **Desired depth:** [quick digest, balanced review, deep summary]
- **Audience:** [founder, engineer, researcher, student]
- **Output artifact:** [weekly digest, reading list, decision memo]

## Your Tasks
1. Restate the topic, audience, and time window
2. Ask Paper Scouts for the strongest recent candidates
3. Ask Summary Analyst for clear paper briefs
4. Ask Trend Editor for cross-paper patterns and hype checks
5. Post the digest plan to Status

workflows.name: Paper Scouting Pass
workflows.description: Find and filter the strongest recent papers in the chosen topic area.
workflows.content:
# Paper Scouting Pass

1. Review the topic, time window, and desired depth defined in kickoff
2. Find the strongest recent papers that are genuinely relevant to the topic
3. Filter out weak matches, low-signal hype, and papers that do not deserve scarce attention
4. Explain why the shortlisted papers matter for the intended audience
5. Post the scouting pass to Scouting and a short status note to Status

workflows.name: Paper Summary Pass
workflows.description: Turn the shortlisted papers into clear summaries with caveats and practical takeaways.
workflows.content:
# Paper Summary Pass

1. Review the shortlisted papers produced by the scouting pass
2. Summarize each paper in plain language with methods, strongest contribution, and practical caveats
3. Distinguish strong evidence from speculative claims or likely overstatement
4. Keep the summaries useful for the audience chosen in kickoff
5. Post the paper summary pass to Summaries and a short status note to Status

workflows.name: Paper Digest Build
workflows.content:
# Paper Digest Build

1. Gather the strongest summaries and papers selected for the digest
2. Identify the major themes, disagreements, and likely overclaims
3. Recommend which papers deserve deeper reading now
4. Post the final digest draft to Digest and the short summary to Status
